graph.name: Modernizing Legacy Applications with Serverless on AWS
graph.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Strangling a legacy Tomcat Java application by migrating features incrementally to serverless Python Lambdas. Shut down 25 on-prem servers and improved user satisfaction by 25%.

nodes.name: Independent Agents
nodes.notes: Agents access the modern single-page application (SPA) to sell insurance policies.

nodes.name: CloudFront CDN
nodes.notes: Caches and serves the static assets of the modernized SPA.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: Hosts the SPA single-page application static files.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Acts as the unified entry point for all modern back-end API requests.

nodes.name: Lambda Authorizer
nodes.notes: Validates user permissions and access rights before hitting back-end services.

nodes.name: Serverless Python Lambda
nodes.notes: Executes backend logic. Scales up and down dynamically. Integrates with DynamoDB and legacy EC2 backend.

nodes.name: Legacy Tomcat Servers
nodes.notes: Legacy Tomcat application hosting non-migrated features like user management.

nodes.name: DynamoDB Cache
nodes.notes: Regional DynamoDB instance used to cache user session state across multiple AZs.

edges.notes: Routes API call to backend API Gateway
Routes validated request to business logic Lambda
Calls legacy Tomcat for non-modernized services
Returns legacy execution result to Python Lambda
Delivers dynamic update back to the agent UI
